text.title: Ops Metrics Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Ops Metrics Basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Set MUST contain 5-10 metrics. Fewer leaves blind spots; more dilutes ownership. Reject configs with > 10 board / weekly metrics.

rationale.source: Andy Grove HBR Output Management; OpenView 2023 SaaS benchmarks

rule.rationale: Cognitive limit on metric reasoning — beyond 10 metrics nobody can hold the system in their head, ownership decays, alerts get ignored.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST have one canonical SQL / formula stored alongside the config. Recomputations across tools forbidden — single source of truth.
rule.rationale: Same name + different formula across reports produces uncomparable numbers + heated meetings. Locked formula prevents drift.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST carry an explicit target value AND an alert threshold. Metrics without targets are observation, not management.
rule.rationale: Without target the metric is a thermometer not a thermostat. Alert threshold makes the metric actionable — change behaviour, not just report.

rule.statement: Cadence MUST be tiered: daily pulse (<= 3 metrics, automated), weekly review (5-10 metrics, owner-led), monthly deep-dive (segments, cohorts). Single weekly-only cadence is forbidden.

rationale.source: Tomasz Tunguz weekly business review; ProfitWell ops cadences

rule.rationale: Tiered cadence matches metric latency. Daily catches fires; weekly catches trends; monthly catches structural shifts. Single cadence misses two of three.

rule.statement: Each metric MUST name a single human owner. Group owners ('marketing', '#growth') are rejected — accountability decays without a name.
rule.rationale: Group-owned metrics rot. Named owner has lever to drive the metric + answer for misses at the review.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
